title: E-theory for $C^\ast$-Categories
author_string: Sarah L. Browne, Paul D. Mitchener
abstract: $E$-theory was originally defined concretely by Connes and Higson and further work followed this construction. We generalise the definition to $C^\ast$-categories. $C^\ast$-categories were formulated to give a theory of operator algebras in a categorical picture and play important role in the study of mathematical physics. In this context, they are analogous to $C^\ast$-algebras and so have invariants defined coming from $C^\ast$-algebra theory but they do not yet have a definition of $E$-theory. Here we define $E$-theory for both complex and real graded $C^\ast$-categories and prove it has similar properties to $E$-theory for $C^\ast$-algebras.
